deep_dive.title: Influence-Guided Symbolic Regression: Scientific Discovery via LLM-Driven Equation Search with Granular Feedback
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a promising avenue for scientific discovery, yet their application to symbolic regression is often constrained by inefficient search strategies and coarse feedback signals. Current methods typically guide LLMs using scalar metrics (e.g., global Mean Squared Error), which fail to identify which components of a proposed equation are driving performance or causing error. We introduce \textit{Influence-Guided Symbolic Regression} (IGSR), a method that frames equation discovery as an iterative two-step process combining diverse term generation with rigorous selection: an LLM generates candidate basis functions $ψ_j(\mathbf{x})$ for a linear model, which are then evaluated using granular influence scores $Δ_j$. These scores quantify each term's marginal contribution to generalization accuracy, enabling an influence-guided pruning process that systematically refines the model structure. Integrating this mechanism into a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) enables navigating the combinatorial search space while balancing exploration of novel functional forms with exploitation of high-influence components. We demonstrate IGSR's effectiveness on a diverse suite of benchmarks, including LLM-SRBench, pharmacological PKPD models, an epidemiological simulation, and real-world genomic data. Notably, we validate the framework's capacity for genuine discovery in a case study using a high-dimensional biological dataset, in which IGSR identified a novel relationship between DNA methylation and RNA Polymerase II pausing; a hypothesis that was subsequently supported via wet-lab experimentation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-driven equation search for automatic discovery of mathematical formulas
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用LLM驱动方程搜索自动发现数学公式
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用LLM驱动方程搜索自动发现数学公式
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用LLM驱动方程搜索自动发现数学公式
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: IGSR uses LLMs to generate candidate basis functions and evaluates them with granular feedback, improving symbolic regression for scientific discovery.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有LLM引导符号回归方法搜索效率低、反馈信号粗糙的问题，提出影响引导的符号回归（IGSR）方法。IGSR将方程发现建模为迭代过程：LLM生成候选基函数，并利用梯度信息提供细粒度反馈。实验表明，IGSR在方程发现任务上优于全局MSE引导的方法，为自动科学发现提供了更有效的范式。

deep_dive.title: The Kalman Evolve: Closing the Gap in Kalman Filtering via Interpretable Algorithm Discovery
deep_dive.abstract: State estimation is a fundamental problem in control and signal processing, for which the Kalman Filter provides an optimal solution under linear dynamics, Gaussian noise, and known noise covariances. However, these assumptions often fail in realistic sensing settings such as Doppler radar and LiDAR. In these cases, the optimal estimator is inherently nonlinear, which leads to systematic performance degradation. This creates a performance gap that cannot be eliminated by tuning the noise covariance parameters (i.e., the process and measurement noise in the Kalman Filter) alone. To address this limitation, we propose Kalman Evolve, a framework for discovering improved filtering algorithms by jointly optimizing both noise parameters and the update structure. Our approach leverages large language models (LLMs) as a structured prior over program space, enabling the generation of interpretable, non-affine modifications to the classical Kalman filter while preserving its recursive form. We provide analytical results establishing the suboptimality of affine estimators under common nonlinear sensing models, motivating the need for structure-aware updates. Across a range of synthetic and real-world tracking benchmarks, including Doppler radar, LiDAR-based localization, and pedestrian tracking, the discovered algorithms consistently improve over strong baselines such as the Optimized Kalman Filter, achieving up to 12\% reduction in RMSE. These results suggest that optimizing the structure of the Kalman filter, rather than only its parameters, provides a practical and interpretable way to improve state estimation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: interpretable algorithm discovery for Kalman filtering
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 可解释的算法发现，应用于卡尔曼滤波改进
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 可解释的算法发现，应用于卡尔曼滤波改进
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 可解释的算法发现，应用于卡尔曼滤波改进
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Kalman Evolve, a framework that discovers improved filtering algorithms by jointly optimizing noise parameters and algorithmic structure, closing the gap in non-ideal settings.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对卡尔曼滤波在非线性、非高斯场景下性能下降的问题，提出Kalman Evolve框架，通过联合优化噪声参数和算法结构来自动发现改进的滤波算法。框架能够产生可解释的新滤波规则，在雷达测距等实际传感任务中显著缩小了与理论最优的差距。

deep_dive.title: Iterative Causal Discovery: Per-Edge Impossibility Certificates, Tier-Aware Oracle Queries, and the $1+K$ Lower Bound
deep_dive.abstract: Causal-discovery algorithms return a directed graph, yet provide no principled means of distinguishing edge directions identified by the data from those assigned without an identifying assumption. Under the standard Markov and faithfulness conditions, the observational distribution identifies only a Markov equivalence class; orientations within that class are not determined by the joint distribution and cannot be recovered from additional samples alone, but require either a functional restriction or an intervention. We introduce a protocol for observational causal discovery on continuous data that attaches to each candidate edge a discrete impossibility certificate: a RESOLVED code records the identifiability theorem under which the direction was committed, while an IMPOSSIBLE code records the failure mode together with the specific question a domain expert must answer to resolve it. The bivariate cascade is extended with five gated identifiability tiers LSNM, IGCI, Stein, MDL, and PEIT that abstain when their precondition test rejects. Two oracle primitives, the meta-hub query and the node-children query, jointly establish an upper bound of $1+K$ expert interactions sufficient to recover any DAG, where $K$ denotes the number of non-leaf vertices. Under an ideal-oracle assumption, the bound is met exactly on the asia, sachs, child, and alarm benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: causal discovery algorithm with impossibility certificates
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 因果发现算法，为每条边提供不可行性证书
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 因果发现算法，为每条边提供不可行性证书
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 因果发现算法，为每条边提供不可行性证书
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a protocol for observational causal discovery that attaches per-edge impossibility certificates, enabling principled distinction between data-identified and assumption-driven orientations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对观察性因果发现中边方向标记无原则的问题，提出一个附带离散不可行性证书（RESOLVED码）的协议。协议使用层感知的Oracle查询，在连续数据上实现边方向的可信度分级，从而提升因果发现的透明性与可验证性。

quick_skim.title: Can Broad Biomedical Knowledge be Contextualized into Scenario-Grounded Propositions?
quick_skim.abstract: Biomedical discovery often requires connecting broad biomedical knowledge with specific experimental or clinical data. Background knowledge suggests relevant mechanisms but is usually too general to map directly onto dataset variables, while data-driven patterns can be dataset-specific and hard to interpret mechanistically. We study this missing link as knowledge contextualization: transforming broad biomedical knowledge into evidence-supported, scenario-grounded propositions that domain experts can inspect, replay, and validate. We propose SCENE, a bi-level multi-agent framework that treats knowledge contextualization as iterative search. The upper level converts broad knowledge into search directions and grounds them in the dataset schema. The lower level executes these directions through multi-objective optimization to identify concrete propositions that balance evidential strength and data support. Feedback between the two levels progressively refines the search. We evaluate SCENE in two settings: discovering patient subgroups with heterogeneous treatment benefits in clinical trial scenarios, and identifying context-specific biological responses in LINCS L1000 studies. In clinical trials, SCENE discovers specific, well-supported subgroups and outperforms existing baselines. In L1000 studies, SCENE identifies perturbational contexts with strong target-response matching and high positive rates. These results show that SCENE bridges broad knowledge and scenario-specific evidence, producing traceable, inspectable hypotheses for follow-up validation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: biomedical knowledge contextualization using iterative search
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用迭代搜索进行生物医学知识上下文化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用迭代搜索进行生物医学知识上下文化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用迭代搜索进行生物医学知识上下文化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SCENE, a bi-level multi-agent framework that iteratively searches and grounds broad biomedical knowledge into scenario-specific propositions, enabling expert validation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 生物医学发现常面临背景知识与具体数据之间的鸿沟。本文提出SCENE，一个双层多智能体框架，将知识上下文化视为迭代搜索过程：上层将宽泛知识转化为搜索方向并锚定到数据集；下层生成可验证的命题。实验表明该方法能有效产生可解释、可复现的假设，助力科学发现。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 我希望去找一些自动发现算法相关的论文，用大模型启发式计算的那种, especially work that connects or combines: algorithms for automatic discovery in data and knowledge; using large language models for discovery tasks; heuristic methods in computational discovery; knowledge discovery with LLM and heuristics; discovery of algorithms using neural networks; symbolic regression with heuristic search; how can large language models be used for automatic discovery of mathematical formulas; heuristic search guided by language models for scientific discovery; automatic discovery algorithms combining LLM and evolutionary computation.

quick_skim.title: UniNote: A Unified Embedding Model for Multimodal Representation and Ranking
quick_skim.abstract: Item-to-Item (I2I) retrieval is a fundamental part of modern content platforms, supporting critical industrial workflows from recommendation engines to content auditing. While multimodal embedding methods have advanced general retrieval, they often falter in I2I scenarios due to the challenges of balancing global content representation with fine-grained local retrieval, the systemic inefficiency of decoupled embedding-and-ranking pipelines, and the inherent trade-offs between model precision and serving latency. To solve these issues, we propose \textbf{UniNote}, a unified embedding model designed for industrial I2I retrieval. Tailored retrieval strategies are introduced to support representation learning over complex, multimodal content at varying granularities. To operationalize these strategies, UniNote employs a two-stage training paradigm: the first stage leverages contrastive SFT to establish robust base embeddings, while the second stage refines ranking quality through a reinforcement learning (RL) process that aligns the model with content relevance. Our results show that UniNote achieves SOTA performance across diverse I2I tasks. Deployed at Xiaohongshu and integrated with Matryoshka Representation Learning (MRL), UniNote achieved significant improvements in retrieval quality and cost efficiency in large-scale applications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Unified embedding model for multimodal ranking with tailored retrieval strategies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于多模态排序的统一嵌入模型，包含定制的检索策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于多模态排序的统一嵌入模型，包含定制的检索策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于多模态排序的统一嵌入模型，包含定制的检索策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: UniNote is a unified embedding model for industrial item-to-item retrieval, balancing global and local representation with efficient serving.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有多模态嵌入模型在商品到商品检索中难以平衡全局与局部表示，且嵌入与排序管线分离导致效率低下。本文提出UniNote统一嵌入模型，通过定制化检索策略支持多模态内容表示，在保持精度同时降低延迟。实验表明，UniNote在工业级检索任务上优于现有方法。
